description: baseline
mold linker (v1.0.3) + clang + target-cpu=native: no compile improvement
sccache: clean build -72% on cache-warm runs (cold=374.8s, warm=100.7s)
sccache+mold: mold adds overhead, worse than sccache-only
strip=debuginfo: compile slightly better (+0.6%) but wall_time 100ms>86ms (16% regression, high noise)
stable toolchain (1.94.1): clean -4.2%, incremental -4.8% vs nightly (warm sccache)
codegen-units=16: link overhead dominates, both metrics worse
codegen-units=8: still worse than 4 due to link overhead
stable+sccache+mold: no compile gain, runtime regression (high noise)
histogram.rs split (566+548 lines): no incremental improvement, bottleneck is linking not compilation
gold linker (-fuse-ld=gold): no improvement over ld.bfd, link/ThinLTO step dominates
sparse registry index: no compile improvement (only affects crate index fetch)
incremental=false: worse incremental (+6.5%), Rust reuses LLVM IR within crate with incremental=true
codegen-units=2: compile -8%/-12% but wall_time regress 100-120ms (threshold 87.8ms)
target-cpu=native: compile +3.6%/+3.8% worse but wall_time -24% (mean ~70ms vs ~92ms)
codegen-units=2+target-cpu=native: compile -4.7%/-7.5%, fitness 0.568 (wall_time 55-85ms, passes guard)
codegen-units=1: slightly worse than 2 (clean +1.3%, incr +0.8%), 2 is sweet spot
cdylib+rlib crate-type: no improvement (cdylib still non-cacheable in sccache)
per-dep codegen-units=16: catastrophically worse (+27%/+35%), ThinLTO slower with more bitcode modules per dep
remove polars-arrow direct dep: neutral (already transitive, same compile time)
prefer-dynamic: incompatible with cdylib (requires static std, build failed)
